stem: A decision-maker must choose among 3 crop selection options. The probability of favorable weather patterns is uncertain, estimated between 36% and 54%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: rice: $261K in good weather patterns, $156K in bad weather patterns. wheat: $138K in good weather patterns, $38K in bad weather patterns. soybeans: $114K in good weather patterns, $51K in bad weather patterns. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.45: best option is rice with EV=$203.2K. rice is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [193.8, 212.7].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
rice is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
